agent_reply: I removed the RAM-loaded/reused PNG buffer path from FlyGui:
- Removed the LittleFS/SdFat image loading helpers from [FlyGui.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp).
The new draw path decodes on demand:
- It gets a compiled sprite via `IconLookup::getSprite()`.
I also removed the remaining LittleFS PNG draw from [SplashView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp), so splash now uses the compiled `sprit_splash` asset and `SpriteDraw`.

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: inc/sprites.h
## My request for Codex:
`MainScreenView` will consist of 6 items, arranged in as 3 columns by 2 rows, starting from the bottom of the top bar

each of these button sprites are 106 x 144 so they should fit, the top row is supposed to be 10 pixels tall, if it is not, make the adjustment, the battery icon right now is not hugging the top as I expect
The top row are sprit_btn_bluetooth sprit_btn_wifi sprit_btn_memo
The bottom row are sprit_btn_smartphone sprit_btn_laptop sprit_btn_wifihome
Make these as generic clickable FlyGuiItem, each with a generic callback
Create 6 new stub functions in "main.cpp" to wire into these callbacks
Also, BtnA, BtnB, BtnC should correspond to touching the bottom 3 icons. So FlyGuiItem probably needs a function that can call the callbacks from an outside caller
agent_reply: - [MainScreenView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp) now lays out 6 sprite-backed `FlyGuiItem`s in a 3x2 grid starting at `y = FlyGui::topBarHeight()`.
- The generated button sprites are actually `106 x 114`, so with a `10px` top bar the grid fits: `10 + 114 + 114 = 238`.
- [FlyGui.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.h) / [FlyGui.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp) now support generic item callbacks, sprite drawing, and `FlyGuiItem::trigger()`.
- BtnA/BtnB/BtnC now activate the bottom-row items: smartphone, laptop, Wi-Fi home.
- Top bar height is now `10`, so the `20x10` battery sprite hugs the top bar vertically.
- Fixed stale `sprit_btn_home` lookup in [IconLookup.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.cpp) to use `sprit_btn_wifihome`.

commit_message: good progress working on GUI but ran into memory problems with PNG decode

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: platformio.ini
## My request for Codex:
originally I designed buttons to be 106x114 sized because it fit a 3x2 layout
then, the RecordingView was designed and I decided on the thick border and for that some 100x100 sized graphics made more sense
and the modal dialogs and error dialogs use... wild sizes
it's kind of a pain in the ass to manage these different sizes

so I added a tool to automatically resize them from original high resolution art with rules about margins and centering and naming, and want to normalize most of the graphics to be 100x100 with only some exceptions
I have regenerated `inc\sprites.h` and `src\sprites.cpp` while I kept `inc\sprites.h.old` as a reference for the replacements
So the logic in `MainScreenView` needs to account for these changes and keep the graphics in the same locations
Various dialog-like views need their top-center graphics changed to use the 100x100 versions, the text position should follow automatically
There are probably other changes that are required
agent_reply: Changed the UI code to use the regenerated 100x100 symbols across:
- Main screen 100x100 graphics are inset inside the old 106x114 grid so their visual centers stay put.
- Modal/error dialog top graphics now use 100x100 assets, with error text positioned from the sprite height instead of a stale hard-coded old graphic size.
- Connection waiting graphics switched from old 180x150 art to 100x100 centered at the old visual center.
- Removed stale references to deleted `btn_*`, `*_180x150`, `error_large`, `exit_50`, and `handshake_60` symbols.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly` succeeds. The only warnings were existing/library warnings, not from these sprite changes.

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: lib/SpriteDraw/SpriteDraw.cpp
## My request for Codex:
we are rewriting how the icons work
the idea of a icon now only applies to user customizable things, such as bluetooth hosts, wifi routers, cloud upload destinations.
The user can select between these
* smartphone
* laptop
* tablet
* home
* cat
* dog
* bird
* circle
* square
* triangle
This should become the new `ICON_*` list
But, these do not directly map to a sprite.
Sprites now support transparency
I have modified `sprite_desc_t`, now any sprite can have an overlay, with enough information for a second call to draw a PNG.
There are three contexts: Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and Cloud
I have modified `IconLookup::getSprite` to take in a context
On the `MainScreenView` bottom row, it should be using `sprite_smartphone_bt_100` and `sprite_laptop_bt_100`, hard code those.
I want the look up to do:
if context is Bluetooth:
* unknown/default = `sprite_unknown_bt_100`
* smartphone = `sprite_smartphone_bt_100`
* laptop = `sprite_laptop_bt_100`
* tablet = `sprite_tablet_bt_100`
* home = `sprite_home_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_bt_50` top right corner
* cat = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cat_50` bottom right corner
* dog = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_dog_50` bottom right corner
* bird = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_bird_50` bottom right corner
* circle = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_square_40` bottom right corner
* square = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_circle_40` bottom right corner
* triangle = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_triangle_40` bottom right corner
if context is Wi-Fi
* unknown/default = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100`
* smartphone = `sprite_smartphone_ap_100`
* laptop = `sprite_laptop_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_wifi_50` top right corner
* tablet = `sprite_tablet_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_wifi_50` top right corner
* home = `sprite_home_wifi_100`
* cat = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cat_50` bottom right corner
* dog = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_dog_50` bottom right corner
* bird = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_bird_50` bottom right corner
* circle = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_square_40` bottom right corner
* square = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_circle_40` bottom right corner
* triangle = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_triangle_40` bottom right corner
if context is Cloud
* unknown/default = `sprite_cloudupload_100`
* smartphone = `sprite_smartphone_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cloud_50` top right corner
* laptop = `sprite_laptop_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cloud_50` top right corner
* tablet = `sprite_tablet_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cloud_50` top right corner
* home = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_home_50` bottom right corner
* cat = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cat_50` bottom right corner
* dog = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_dog_50` bottom right corner
* bird = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_bird_50` bottom right corner
* circle = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_square_40` bottom right corner
* square = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_circle_40` bottom right corner
* triangle = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_triangle_40` bottom right corner
agent_reply: The new user-selectable `ICON_*` list is now only: unknown, smartphone, laptop, tablet, home, cat, dog, bird, circle, square, triangle in [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:53).

`IconLookup::getSprite()` is now context-aware in [IconLookup.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.cpp:427), with the Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and Cloud mappings you specified, including overlay placement. I kept the legacy string aliases like `"phone"` mapping to `ICON_SMARTPHONE` so old JSON does not become useless.

`FlyGuiItem` now supports `sprite_desc_t` overlays and draws the overlay as a second PNG call in [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:485). `ScrollView` now uses context lookup for user-configurable entries, while built-in actions like pairing, scan, info, and NTP use hard-coded sprites instead of fake semantic icons.
Main screen bottom-row phone/laptop buttons now use the Bluetooth variants directly in [MainScreenView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:54).

I also bumped the secure NVS config versions and added legacy icon migration so old stored values like old `ICON_WIFI` do not accidentally become one of the new user icons. During build, I hit the existing secure `cloud_item_t` mismatch and restored the cloud password field because the rest of the firmware still depends on it.
The new user-selectable `ICON_*` list is now only: unknown, smartphone, laptop, tablet, home, cat, dog, bird, circle, square, triangle in [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:53).

`IconLookup::getSprite()` is now context-aware in [IconLookup.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.cpp:427), with the Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and Cloud mappings you specified, including overlay placement. I kept the legacy string aliases like `"phone"` mapping to `ICON_SMARTPHONE` so old JSON does not become useless.

`FlyGuiItem` now supports `sprite_desc_t` overlays and draws the overlay as a second PNG call in [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:485). `ScrollView` now uses context lookup for user-configurable entries, while built-in actions like pairing, scan, info, and NTP use hard-coded sprites instead of fake semantic icons.
Main screen bottom-row phone/laptop buttons now use the Bluetooth variants directly in [MainScreenView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:54).

I also bumped the secure NVS config versions and added legacy icon migration so old stored values like old `ICON_WIFI` do not accidentally become one of the new user icons. During build, I hit the existing secure `cloud_item_t` mismatch and restored the cloud password field because the rest of the firmware still depends on it.
